perturbation.original_text: 8. No Trading on Inside Information . Company is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty, of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities by any person who has received material non-public information from ZipRealty or its Representatives and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person may purchase or sell ZipRealty’s securities while in possession of such information. Company agrees to require its Representatives to comply with these restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. No Trading on Inside Information . Company is aware of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities. Company agrees to require its Representatives to comply with these restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit statement that 'Company will advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty' about insider trading restrictions, the contract creates an ambiguity. The Company is still responsible for the Representatives' compliance but loses the defined method of informing them about the rules. This omission in section 8 creates an in-text contradiction by weakening the means to enforce section 3, regarding nondisclosure of information, making compliance of the Representatives, with this regard, ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . ZipRealty and Company agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except the Permitted Purpose. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or nonpermitted use of Confidential Information of the other party in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures will include the highest degree of care that either party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 8 explicitly states the 'Company will advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty', ensuring they are informed about insider trading restrictions. The changed text removes this explicit obligation, only stating 'Company is aware of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities'. This omission weakens the means by which the Company ensures compliance with section 3, regarding non-disclosure of information. Section 3 requires each party to ensure its Representatives maintain confidentiality, and to take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information. By removing the explicit requirement to inform Representatives about insider trading restrictions, the modified section 8 creates an in-text contradiction, making enforcement of section 3 regarding Representatives' compliance ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 10. No Recruitment of Other Party’s Employees . (a) No Company Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of ZipRealty set forth on Exhibit A, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with ZipRealty, provided that general, non-targeted advertisements or solicitations through independent employment recruiters will not be deemed to breach the foregoing provisions of this sentence. (b) No ZipRealty Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of Company set forth on Exhibit B, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with Company, provided that general, non-targeted advertisements or solicitations through independent employment recruiters will not be deemed to breach the foregoing provisions of this sentence.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. No Recruitment of Other Party’s Employees . (a) No Company Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of ZipRealty set forth on Exhibit A, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with ZipRealty. (b) No ZipRealty Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of Company set forth on Exhibit B, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'provided that general, non-targeted advertisements or solicitations through independent employment recruiters will not be deemed to breach the foregoing provisions of this sentence' clause in both (a) and (b), the agreement now prohibits even general job postings that might attract employees. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because it now conflicts with standard business practices and reasonable expectations. The explicit prohibition contradicts implicitly accepted industry practices making this section contradictory to reasonable business conduct. Although this is not addressed in the document, the very nature of this perturbation, creates an in-text contradiction with general knowledge.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. No Recruitment of Other Party’s Employees . (a) No Company Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of ZipRealty set forth on Exhibit A, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with ZipRealty. (b) No ZipRealty Representative with knowledge of the potential business opportunity being explored will, directly or indirectly, recruit or solicit for hire any of the employees of Company set forth on Exhibit B, or aid anyone else in recruiting or soliciting for hire any of such employees, or induce or otherwise encourage any of such employees to leave their employment with Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for general advertisements or solicitations through independent employment recruiters. The changed text removes this exception, meaning any form of recruitment, even general job postings, could be considered a breach. This creates a direct conflict because, without the exception, standard business practices now arguably violate the agreement. The 'Contradicted Text' is exactly the same as the 'Changed Text', since the contradiction is based on the removal of the exception clause. This removal creates an in-text contradiction by conflicting with industry practices.

perturbation.original_text: 11. No Conflicting Obligations . Each disclosing party hereby represents and warrants that it may rightfully disclose the Confidential Information to the receiving party, and enter into the potential business opportunity being explored, in each case without violating any contractual, legal, fiduciary or other obligation to any person. The representing party shall indemnify and hold harmless the other party against any damages, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) incurred in connection with any misrepresentation by the representing party in the foregoing sentence.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No Conflicting Obligations . Each disclosing party hereby represents and warrants that it may rightfully disclose the Confidential Information to the receiving party, and enter into the potential business opportunity being explored, in each case without violating any contractual, legal, fiduciary or other obligation to any person.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence regarding indemnification ('The representing party shall indemnify and hold harmless the other party against any damages, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) incurred in connection with any misrepresentation by the representing party in the foregoing sentence.'), the clause still warrants against conflicting obligations, but provides no remedy if that warranty is breached. This makes the representation less enforceable and contradicts the implicit expectation of redress for misrepresentation, making the utility of the entire clause ambiguous. Removing the indemnification clause creates an in-text contradiction by removing the remediation from breach of warrant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. No Conflicting Obligations . Each disclosing party hereby represents and warrants that it may rightfully disclose the Confidential Information to the receiving party, and enter into the potential business opportunity being explored, in each case without violating any contractual, legal, fiduciary or other obligation to any person.
perturbation.justification: The original text's Clause 11 included an indemnification provision to protect the receiving party from damages resulting from misrepresentation by the disclosing party regarding conflicting obligations. The changed text removes this indemnification clause. The contradicted text is the original, unedited Clause 11, which includes the indemnification clause. The removal of the indemnification clause creates a contradiction because while the disclosing party still warrants no conflicting obligations, the receiving party now has no clear recourse or remedy if this warranty is breached. This lack of remedy directly contradicts the implicit expectation of redress established by the original, unedited Clause 11, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Remedies . Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Remedies . Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without the necessity of proving actual damages' from the remedies section, the party seeking injunctive relief is now required to prove actual damages, even though the agreement acknowledges monetary damages may be inadequate. This requirement contradicts the initial acknowledgement that monetary damages may not suffice. Although equitable relief may be sought, now proof of damages is required, making the entire clause contradictory with itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Remedies . Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'without the necessity of proving actual damages'. The original text explicitly states that actual damages do not need to be proven to seek injunctive relief. The changed text, by removing this phrase, implicitly requires proof of actual damages, thereby contradicting the original intent and creating an in-text contradiction within section 14 as well as contradicting the acknowledgement that monetary damages may be inadequate, impacting the enforceability of the remedies clause. The contradicted text is the original section 14, which no longer aligns with the altered section 14 due to the omission.
